title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search · Mastery Check
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for bisect, reproducible diagnosis, and automated regression search and requires the smallest safe reproducible bisect harness and causal report with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reproducible bisect harness and causal report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.
Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Bisect Search Invariant evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Bisect Search Invariant assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G32 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Bisect Search Invariant has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Bisect Search Invariant precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G32 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Bisect tests commits in timestamp order and always needs exactly log2(n) trials.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Bisect Cause Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G39 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reproduce the boundary, inspect the candidate diff and dependencies, revert or patch experimentally, retain a regression test, and distinguish correlation from cause.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
